text:
A small town bird had a tiny dream once. She dreamed she could fly up to the stars. Every night she'd chirp dreamily and flap her wings right beside the night sky.

Her family laughed at her. "Fly? Birds only live here!"

But she didn't give up on her lofty ambition. She saved up her song feathers, learned to weave silken threads into her flights, and hung them from her beak like tiny stars.

One spring night, as she practiced her dreamy tunes by the moon, a gentle breeze carried away a feather.

A starry owl heard her sorrow. "Fly to me," it whispered.

She took off without a feather in sight. Weeks passed, and her dreams faded. Then she saw a shooting star.

Her heart leaped. "Come back!" she cried.

The star twinkled. "I'm always watching."

They flew together—a little bird with dreams and a wise owl. They met other birds with peculiar talents—snakes
